test_inputs.name: deep-research-unfamiliar-topic
test_inputs.prompt: /research deep the psychology of career indecision in high-achievers — why do successful people get stuck choosing what's next?
test_inputs.expected_behavior: Full 5-phase deep research at 4,000-8,000 words with domain map, all 5 layers, confidence labels, self-audit, and shelf-life markers
success_criteria: Runs brainstorm gate: restates question, sharpens angle, surfaces assumptions
Reads sam.md for user context before starting
Checks /research/outputs/ for prior art
Produces domain map (3-8 sentences) before any source gathering
Uses at least 3 different search channels and conducts 8+ searches
Sources organized by tier with quality signals assessed
All 5 analysis layers present: Foundations, Evidence, Tactics, Contrarian, So What
Every substantive claim has a confidence label (1-5 star scale)
Self-audit section present addressing all 8 checks
Shelf-life markers assigned (Durable/Monitor/Perishable)
Source log table with tier, found-via, and contribution columns
Saved to /research/outputs/[slug].md
No unverified training-data claims presented as sourced fact
Counter-evidence actively searched for major claims

test_inputs.name: quick-brief
test_inputs.prompt: /research quick evidence-based negotiation tactics for salary discussions
test_inputs.expected_behavior: Research brief at 1,500-2,500 words with domain map, top findings, top tactics, and source log
success_criteria: Output is 1,500-2,500 words (not deep research length)
Domain map present (3-5 sentences)
3-5 key findings with confidence labels
3-5 practical tactics tied to evidence
'What I'd Want to Know More About' section present
Source log with top sources
Uses at least 2 search channels and 5+ searches
Saved with -brief suffix
Confidence labels present on all findings
Shelf-life marker assigned

test_inputs.name: contrarian-analysis
test_inputs.prompt: /research contrarian the benefits of remote work — steelman the case that return-to-office is actually better for most companies
test_inputs.expected_behavior: Focused contrarian/minority view analysis steelmanning RTO arguments with evidence
success_criteria: Addresses all 5 contrarian dimensions: replication concerns, practitioner-academic gaps, emerging counter-evidence, structural critiques, credible outlier perspectives
Each contrarian position presented in its strongest form (steelmanned)
Every claim confidence-labeled
Does not manufacture dissent — if some dimensions have no credible contrarian position, says so
Sources actual counter-evidence rather than hypothetical arguments
Distinguishes between popular contrarian talking points and evidence-backed challenges
Saved with -contrarian suffix

test_inputs.name: domain-map
test_inputs.prompt: /research map behavioral economics and decision-making under uncertainty
test_inputs.expected_behavior: 500-1,000 word domain map covering key researchers, institutions, debates, texts, and field direction
success_criteria: Output is 500-1,000 words (orientation level, not full research)
Covers: key researchers and institutions
Covers: major schools of thought / competing frameworks
Covers: foundational texts
Covers: major debates and open questions
Covers: where the field is heading
Saved with -map suffix
Useful for deciding what to research in depth next — surfaces choice points

test_inputs.name: tactics-extraction
test_inputs.prompt: /research tactics managing energy (not time) for sustained deep work — specific protocols, not theory
test_inputs.expected_behavior: Actionable tactics grounded in evidence, skipping theory, with difficulty and impact labels
success_criteria: Checks /research/outputs/ for existing research on the topic
Tactics are concrete enough to execute (specific protocols, not platitudes)
Each tactic tied to evidence with source attribution
Each tactic labeled for implementation difficulty and expected impact
Each tactic confidence-labeled
No extended theory sections — tactics-first structure
Saved with -tactics suffix
At least 5 distinct, implementable tactics
Named sources with specifics, not 'studies show'
